summary: Deterministic M4 replay fixture now covers objective creation through muster, supply, route, march, automatic engagement/siege, withdrawal, and postwar candidate creation. Node and worker-compatible execution paths produce identical M4 hashes and tail hashes. M4 save requests are explicitly rejected, and loading an M4 runtime from a snapshot without M4 state fails with a semantic-invariant error at state.m4.

tests_run.result: PASS; showed only the expected modified files in allowed paths.
PASS; Node and worker-compatible M4 hashes matched at 232f62ab, with stable engagement, siege, withdrawal, and postwar candidate outputs.
PASS; root vitest 33 files / 239 tests and web package 1 file / 3 tests.
PASS; formatting, lint, typecheck, and architecture checks succeeded after fixing the touched files.
PASS; task state validated as REVIEW.
PASS; task JSON shape and REVIEW routing validated.
PASS; no whitespace or patch-format issues.

decisions: Deterministic M4 replay coverage is implemented as an explicit command sequence and verified across Node and worker-compatible paths using the same fixture.
M4 save-format persistence is intentionally rejected with a precise compatibility error instead of expanding the save schema within this task boundary.
The browser worker host hash canary now includes M4 replay hashes so the deterministic path is covered outside the package-level tests as well.
risks: M4 runtime persistence remains unsupported in save-format v1 and will continue to reject M4 save requests until a future task extends the schema on purpose.
The replay fixture is intentionally narrow to the acceptance criteria and does not introduce unrelated gameplay, UI, networking, telemetry, or modding behavior.
requested_action: Review the deterministic M4 replay fixture, Node/worker parity evidence, and the explicit M4 save/load compatibility boundary.
